title: Same Tree
problem_statement: Determine if two binary trees are structurally identical and contain the same values in corresponding nodes.

examples.explanation: Both trees have the same structure and values.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 100]
intuition: Traverse both trees in sync and verify that every corresponding pair of nodes matches in value and structure.

approaches.name: Recursive subtree comparison
approaches.description: Check both nodes for nullity, compare values, and recurse on left and right pairs.
approaches.why_not: Serializing trees to strings can be done, but recursion is clearer and avoids string edge cases.
interview_commentary: Compare both trees in parallel by visiting corresponding nodes.
Return false as soon as any mismatch in structure or value appears.
Both null nodes indicate identical empty subtrees.

interview_qa.q: What is the first comparison at each node?
interview_qa.a: Check whether both nodes are null, or one is null, or their values differ.

interview_qa.q: How do you compare child subtrees?
interview_qa.a: Recursively compare left children together and right children together.

interview_qa.q: What happens if one tree is larger?
interview_qa.a: The comparison returns false when a subtree exists in one tree but not the other.
Yes, with two stacks or queues to traverse both trees in parallel.
